mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals significant slack in the labor market. This slack suppresses wage inflation and ensures the central bank maintains an accommodative monetary policy, lowering discount rates and supporting higher equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 9.6%, well above typical full-employment levels.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is near the zero-lower-bound, it provides maximum policy accommodation. This ultra-low rate environment reduces borrowing costs for corporations and lowers the discount rate for future earnings, directly boosting equity prices.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint weakness in housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This interaction signals imminent downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in HOUST < 0 AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: Instances where both HOUST and RSAFS are declining over 3 months are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production following a major economic downturn confirms that the supply side of the economy is healing. This 'green shoot' is a leading indicator of a durable recovery in GDP and corporate profits, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.19 is below its 2017 baseline of 100, suggesting recovery potential.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO has a Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high unemployment creates negative sentiment and uncertainty about the pace of economic recovery. This uncertainty increases the equity risk premium demanded by investors, causing short-term negative pressure on the S&P 500.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 9.0%, the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpected increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) signal rising inflation. This forces the market to price in a higher probability of future monetary tightening by the central bank, which compresses valuation multiples and leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is not negatively correlated with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When strong payroll growth occurs simultaneously with stagnant or falling real GDP, it implies a sharp decline in labor productivity. This dynamic compresses corporate profit margins as labor costs rise without a corresponding increase in output, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly change in PAYEMS > 0 AND quarterly change in GDPC1 < 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 50% of quarters where payrolls rise but real GDP falls.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) is a high-frequency indicator of consumer health. A negative month-over-month change indicates weakening consumer spending, which directly impacts revenues for a large portion of the S&P 500 and leads to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 3-year rolling window.
